generation.method: exact scheduler npm tarball plus local scheduler implementation copied under an isolated alias into a temporary node_modules tree
generation.probeIsolation: one Node child process per target, scenario, and mode
generation.timingNormalization: scheduler/unstable_mock virtual time is recorded directly; raw wall-clock timestamps and local filesystem paths are omitted; local package metadata is observed but omitted from behavior comparison

afterWorker120.source: packages/scheduler/unstable_mock.js and cjs/scheduler-unstable_mock.* are copied under fast-react-scheduler for behavior comparison

probeModes.reason: scheduler/unstable_mock selects cjs/scheduler-unstable_mock.development.js when NODE_ENV is not production.
scheduler/unstable_mock selects cjs/scheduler-unstable_mock.production.js when NODE_ENV is production.

captures: disable-yield-value behavior for logs and virtual time

scenarios.area: Task object shape and virtual timeout buckets

captures: wrapCallback priority capture with this and argument forwarding
ready callback execution order across public priorities
pending-work state before and after flushing

scenarios.area: Flush helper return and assertion behavior
Pending, delayed, expired, and cancelled work
captures: ready work flushes before delayed work
advanceTime moves delayed work into pending work
callback return function is stored as a continuation
reset clears virtual time, log, and scheduled callback handles
reset with pending work clears scheduled handles but stale queue state can keep fresh equal-priority work non-flushable

packageJson.name: scheduler
packageJson.description: Cooperative scheduler for the browser environment.

fastReactScheduler.source: local packages/scheduler copied for behavior comparison

implementationRisks: The local scheduler package metadata intentionally remains workspace-specific and is excluded from scheduler/unstable_mock behavior comparisons.
The oracle intentionally covers the mock scheduler only; root scheduler, post-task, native, reconciler lane scheduling, and renderer behavior remain separate compatibility surfaces.
Future upstream React-style tests still depend on harness aliasing and environment setup outside this public scheduler mock behavior surface.

flushAllWithPreExistingLog.name: Error
flushAllWithPreExistingLog.message: Log is not empty. Assert on the log of yielded values before flushing additional work.

flushAllWithYieldedValue.name: Error
flushAllWithYieldedValue.message: While flushing work, something yielded a value. Use an assertion helper to assert on the log of yielded values, e.g. expect(Scheduler).toFlushAndYield([...])

events.name: Error
events.message: Cannot reset while already flushing work.
